content.full_text:
Re:
Fern Polrrrcnl PRACTTcEs CouurssroN
428 J Street o Suite 620 o Sacramento, CA g5gl4-2329
(916) 322-5660 o Fax (916) 322-0886
November 3,2009
Gary L. Svanda
173 North Park Drive
Madera, California 93637
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-09-224
Dear Mr. Svanda:
This letter responds to your rqquest for advice regarding the filing provisions of
the Political Reform Act (the "Act").r The Act requires the Commission tb provide
written advice to any person whose duties under the Act are in question or to that
person's authorized representative. (Section 83l la(b); Regulation 1g329(a), copy
enclosed.) However, the Commission may decline to provide written advice if it would
be inappropriate or otherwise not in the public interest to do so. (Regulation
1832e(b)(8XF).)
Formal written advice is the application of law to a given set of facts provided by
the requestor and has the purpose of conferring immunity to the requestor. (Section
831 14; Regulation 18329(b)(7).) After a careful review of the facts you provided, we
decline to provide written advice to you at this time. The same or ue.y ri-ilur issues are
currently the subject of a pending complaint in the Commission's Enforcement Division.
Please note that our decision is not a determination of the questions posed in your
request. In addition, nothing in the Act prevents you from seeking legal advice frornyour
agency's counsel or private counsel.
I The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code sections g 1000 through gl014. All
statutory references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulationi of the Fair
Political Practices Commission are contained in Sections I tl I I0 through l8gg7 of Tiile 2 of the California
Code of Regulations. All regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of
Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.
€i?


File No. W-09-224
Page No. 2
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
Counsel
By: William
Senior
el, Legal Division
WJL:jgl

sections.conclusion: After a careful review of the facts you provided, we decline to provide written advice to you at this time. The same or very similar issues are currently the subject of a pending complaint in the Commission's Enforcement Division.
sections.question_synthetic: The requestor sought advice regarding filing provisions and duties under the California Political Reform Act, but the specific details of the question are not included in this declination letter.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declined to provide written advice because the same or very similar issues are the subject of a pending complaint in the Enforcement Division, which makes it inappropriate to provide formal written advice at this time.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a declination letter (File No. W-09-224). The specific questions posed by the requestor are not reproduced in this document; only the FPPC's response declining to provide advice is included. The letter references pending enforcement proceedings as the reason for declining.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: The requestor sought advice regarding filing provisions and duties under the California Political Reform Act, but the specific details of the question are not included in this declination letter.

CONCLUSION: After a careful review of the facts you provided, we decline to provide written advice to you at this time. The same or very similar issues are currently the subject of a pending complaint in the Commission's Enforcement Division.

embedding.summary: The FPPC declined to provide written advice to Gary L. Svanda regarding Political Reform Act filing provisions because the issues raised are currently the subject of a pending enforcement complaint. The declining decision does not constitute a determination of the questions posed.
